candidates.authoritative_state_model: Single embedded SQLite database file per session; canonical entities stored relationally with explicit BEGIN/COMMIT boundaries; derived cache rows stored separately and rebuildable.
candidates.durable_acknowledgement_boundary: Authoritative command returns Ok only after SQLite transaction COMMIT succeeds.
candidates.single_writer_strategy: Dedicated writer_lock table row plus open writable handle enforcement; second writer receives writer-already-open.
candidates.stale_write_strategy: Scoped semantic preconditions validated against loaded canonical state before mutation.
candidates.format_version_strategy: session_meta.format_version integer; unknown newer versions reject writable open.
candidates.corruption_behavior: SQLite integrity check plus semantic reload validation; canonical corruption fails closed on writable open.
candidates.duplication_behavior: New session directory and database copied under new session identity with duplicated_from lineage metadata.
candidates.compaction_gc_behavior: Optional VACUUM-based compaction rewrites database file while preserving semantic oracle equivalence.
candidates.backup_boundary: Filesystem copy of session.db tested only as spike control; not a product backup feature.
platform_dependencies: POSIX/Windows file locking semantics via SQLite
known_limitations: Spike uses simplified relational layout, not production schema
Destructive historical GC not implemented in spike
Fault injection is logical-layer only; not hardware power-loss equivalence
candidates.mechanism_class: Append-oriented event/log storage with versioned bundle directory
candidates.authoritative_state_model: Append-only JSONL canonical log plus generation manifest and optional checkpoint snapshots; derived index files stored separately.
candidates.durable_acknowledgement_boundary: Authoritative command returns Ok only after append record fsync and manifest generation bump via atomic rename.
candidates.single_writer_strategy: writer.lock token file with epoch counter; stale ownership requires explicit takeover with integrity reload.
candidates.stale_write_strategy: Same scoped semantic preconditions as embedded candidate; enforced before append record is written.
candidates.format_version_strategy: manifest.format_version; unknown newer manifest rejects writable open.
candidates.corruption_behavior: Log replay validates record chain; truncated or invalid canonical records fail closed.
candidates.duplication_behavior: New bundle directory with copied canonical log tail and independent writer.lock epoch.
candidates.compaction_gc_behavior: Optional generation rewrite compacts log into new checkpoint generation with atomic directory swap.
candidates.backup_boundary: Directory copy tested as mechanism-specific control only.
known_limitations: No OS-level crash injection in spike
Destructive historical GC not implemented in spike
Windows directory fsync behavior recorded separately
Fault injection is logical-layer only; not hardware power-loss equivalence

material_distinctness_summary: Candidate A delegates durability/recovery partly to embedded transactional engine with relational constraints. Candidate B uses append-dominant authoritative log, explicit manifest generations, and replay/checkpoint recovery without a database engine.
